themes.label: Fed Hike Repricing — The Q3 Macro Axis
themes.narrative: After the hawkish June dots (9 members projecting at least one 2026 hike) and a strong June jobs print, the market prices roughly two-thirds odds of a September hike. The story: the economy is too hot, the cutting cycle is dead, real rates stay up.
themes.dataAnchor: Real 10Y yield sits at 2.23% — already restrictive. Yet the stress gauges disagree with the panic: HY OAS is 2.72% (tight, no credit stress), VIX 16.1, and the 2s10s curve is +0.35 and re-steepened. Fed funds is 3.63% with the 2Y at 4.13% — the front end has already done the hiking for the Fed.
themes.counterRead: Hike pricing at ~2/3 odds is a crowded macro bet that one soft CPI (Jul 14) or NFP (Aug 7) unwinds violently. With credit tight and no stress anywhere, the asymmetry is in fading the repricing through the assets it crushed — monetary metals — with defined-risk tranches, not in chasing the dollar trade. Everything gold and silver do between now and Sep 16 is downstream of this single repricing.
themes.watchFor: CME FedWatch September hike odds dropping below 40% after the Jul 14 CPI or Aug 7 NFP.

themes.label: Gold & Silver — Correction Within a Structural Bull
themes.narrative: Gold -27% from the Jan 29 record, silver -52%. The narrative says the metals trade is over: hawkish Fed, strong dollar, fading geopolitical premium.
themes.dataAnchor: Gold prints $4,086/oz — even after the correction it sits at the ~94th percentile of its 3-year range (monetary-metal lens: SCARCE). Central banks bought ~850t in 2025, third-highest on record, and they buy dips. Jun 24 was a capitulation low on 2-3x volume (IAU 74.44, SIVR 52.90). Bank targets sit far above spot: GS $5,400, JPM $6,000, ING Q3 average $4,300.
themes.counterRead: The 2008 analog (gold -30%, then +180% into 2011) is conditional — that leg required the Fed easing. The cautionary analog is 2011-2015: -45% over four years under a hawkish Fed. What breaks the tie now: central banks, not Western investors, are the marginal buyer. The trigger to get aggressive is Fed repricing, not a price level — tranche plan and scenario map live in data/intelligence/metals-hedge-state.json.
themes.watchFor: Gold rallying on hawkish news — seller exhaustion is the most reliable bottom tell.

themes.label: AI Infrastructure Hardware — Rate Compression, Not Demand Break
themes.narrative: QQQ is -4.4% over one month while IWM is +1.1% — the market is rotating out of mega-cap AI beta as if the AI capex cycle is rolling over.
themes.dataAnchor: AVGO sits -25% from its 52-week high (FULL_SIGNAL, decline tagged SENTIMENT_DRIVEN, RSI 43.8) and NVDA is -10.4% on the month — while credit stays tight (HY OAS 2.72%) and no tracked AI-infrastructure name shows a downward revenue inflection. This is multiple compression from the rate repricing, not a demand break.
themes.counterRead: The same rate shock pressing gold is compressing AI hardware multiples — one macro trade wearing two costumes. If September resolves dovish, both re-rate together. AVGO at -25% with intact fundamentals is the highest-quality dislocation on the board.
themes.watchFor: NVDA Aug 27 print — actual order-book deterioration would change this from DATA_AHEAD to ALIGNED.

themes.label: NAVN — Profitability Inflection, Parabolic Price
themes.narrative: Navan is the broken-IPO redemption story: $28 IPO to $8.10 March low to a $28.22 all-time high on Jul 7. Two straight surprise-profit quarters, the Smartrips acquisition, Strong Buy consensus. The narrative says the re-rating is just starting.
themes.dataAnchor: The profitability inflection is real: Q1 FY27 EPS $0.08 vs -$0.02 expected, gross bookings above $3B, 11% non-GAAP operating margin, FY27 revenue guide ~$910M (+30%). But price ran +30% in three weeks to ~7x forward sales and the company is not yet GAAP-profitable. Scanner reads RSI 62.9 at -9.6% from high: nearTrough=false, no entry signal.
themes.counterRead: Fundamentals turned, but price has front-run them near-term — the mirror image of where the scanner caught MU. Watch for digestion, don't chase: interest zones 24.80 (Jul 6 low), 23.30 (Jul 2 low), 22 (June base). The ~Aug 12 Q2 FY27 print is the binary event — first quarter defending the new profitability.
themes.watchFor: NAVN holding the 23-24 zone into the ~Aug 12 print without losing the June base at 22.

themes.label: Midterm-Year Seasonality — The Q4 Setup
themes.narrative: Common read: election uncertainty means the correction comes after the Nov 3 midterms, so stay defensive into year-end.
themes.dataAnchor: History inverts that: since 1962 the S&P 500 has never been negative in the 12 months following a midterm (average +12-16%). Midterm years bottom in the Sep-Oct pre-election window as uncertainty peaks, and the rally historically starts ~22 trading days before election day. The Oct 27-28 FOMC lands three business days before the vote — the Fed will not act there, which makes Sep 16 the decision that sets the entire Q4 path.
themes.counterRead: The pre-election window (September through late October) is where dislocations get bought, not sold. Dry powder deployed into that window — metals tranches, AVGO-class dislocations, NAVN digestion — is positioned in front of the historically strongest 12-month stretch of the four-year cycle.
themes.watchFor: VIX above 25 in the Sep-Oct window with the hike question already resolved — the historical buy-signal combination.

strategyPosture: Hold core winners; do not add QQQ-beta on margin into the hike repricing. Begin metals tranche 1 (IAU 74.50-76.50 zone) — the portfolio is 100% risk-on with zero defensive assets and ~13% crypto-linked exposure. NAVN is Watch, not Add, above 24.
whereWaveBuilds: Monetary metals into the Sep 16 FOMC: either a soft print kills hike odds (bottom already in at IAU 74.44) or the hike lands and the flush to IAU 67-72 marks the low, per the Dec-2015 first-hike analog. The pre-midterm Sep-Oct window is the deployment zone for everything on the dislocation board.
watchFor: Jul 14 CPI: core soft enough to drop FedWatch September hike odds below 40%
Aug 7 NFP: the fastest lever on hike odds in either direction
Gold rallying on hawkish news — seller exhaustion / bottom confirmation
IAU June 24 low (74.44): holds = capitulation confirmed; breaks = scenario C toward 67-72
NAVN 23-24 zone ahead of the ~Aug 12 Q2 FY27 print
